questions.question_text: A property owner sells their own home without hiring an agent. Does this require a real estate license in Vermont?
distractors: Yes, all sales require a license
Only if the owner advertises online
questions.explanation: Property owners selling their own real estate are exempt from Vermont licensing requirements. A license is required only when someone lists, sells, buys, or manages property for compensation on behalf of another.

questions.question_text: An attorney handles real estate transactions as part of administering a deceased client's estate. Must the attorney hold a real estate license?
distractors: Yes, any sale requires a license
Only if the property value exceeds $250,000
Only if the attorney collects a commission
questions.explanation: Attorneys acting in connection with estate, trust, or bankruptcy matters are exempt from Vermont real estate licensing. This exemption recognizes the attorney's existing professional regulation.

questions.question_text: Which activity requires a Vermont real estate license?
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, buying, or managing real property for compensation on behalf of another requires a Vermont real estate license. Selling your own property, court-appointed roles, and appraisal are exempt.

questions.question_text: A court appoints a receiver to manage and sell foreclosed properties. Does this person need a real estate license?
distractors: Yes, all property sales require a license
Only if the receiver earns a commission
Only if the receiver is not an attorney
questions.explanation: Court-appointed receivers are exempt from Vermont licensing requirements. The court appointment provides oversight that substitutes for the consumer protections of licensure.

questions.question_text: A public officer conducts a tax sale of abandoned property. Is a license required?
distractors: Yes, all sales need a license
Only if the officer profits personally
Only if the sale is over $50,000
questions.explanation: Public officers performing their official duties related to real estate are exempt from Vermont licensing requirements. Tax sales conducted by government officials fall under this exemption.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a salesperson license in Vermont?
questions.explanation: Vermont requires salesperson applicants to be at least 18 years of age. Additional requirements include completing 40 hours of pre-license education, passing the exam, and a background check.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to upgrade to a broker license. What experience requirement must be met?
questions.explanation: To qualify for a broker license in Vermont, an applicant must have at least 2 years of experience as a licensed salesperson, plus complete 40 additional hours of broker education.

questions.question_text: A brokerage firm opens a second location. What is required for this new office?
distractors: A separate broker license for each agent
questions.explanation: Vermont requires a branch office license for each additional office location operated by a brokerage. This ensures each location is properly supervised and registered.

questions.question_text: How many additional education hours must a salesperson complete to qualify for a broker license?
questions.explanation: Vermont broker applicants must complete 40 additional hours of broker-level education beyond the salesperson pre-license requirement. This is in addition to 2 years of salesperson experience.

questions.question_text: An applicant has completed pre-license education and passed the exam. What additional step is required before licensure?
distractors: A personal interview with the Commission
questions.explanation: Vermont requires salesperson applicants to pass a background check in addition to completing 40 hours of pre-license education and passing the licensing exam.

questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education does Vermont require for a salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Vermont requires 40 hours of approved pre-license education for salesperson applicants. This must be completed before sitting for the licensing examination.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson must complete post-license education. Within what timeframe?
questions.explanation: Vermont requires newly licensed salespersons to complete 8 hours of post-license education within 90 days of receiving their license.

questions.question_text: How many continuing education hours must a Vermont salesperson complete each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Vermont salespersons must complete 16 hours of continuing education every 2-year renewal cycle, including 4 hours of mandatory topics designated by the Commission.

questions.question_text: A Vermont broker is preparing for license renewal. How many CE hours are required per cycle?
questions.explanation: Vermont brokers must complete 24 hours of continuing education every 2-year renewal cycle, which is 8 more hours than the salesperson requirement of 16 hours.

questions.question_text: Of the 16 CE hours required for salesperson renewal, how many must cover mandatory topics?
questions.explanation: Vermont salespersons must complete 4 hours of mandatory topics as part of their 16-hour CE requirement. The remaining 12 hours may be in elective subjects.

questions.question_text: When is the biennial renewal date for Vermont real estate licenses?
questions.explanation: Vermont real estate licenses renew biennially on May 31. Licensees must complete required CE and pay renewal fees by this date to maintain active status.

questions.question_text: What is the license renewal fee for a Vermont real estate licensee?
questions.explanation: The Vermont real estate license renewal fee is $220. Other fees include $100 for the initial application and $110 for the licensing exam.

questions.question_text: A salesperson switches to a new brokerage firm. What must be done with OPR?
questions.explanation: When a salesperson changes brokers, they must notify the Office of Professional Regulation of the change. The license transfers without requiring a new exam or additional education.

questions.question_text: A licensee let their license expire two renewal cycles ago. What may be required for reinstatement?
distractors: Only a written apology to the Commission
questions.explanation: When a Vermont license has been expired for an extended period, the licensee may need to meet CE requirements, pay reinstatement fees, and potentially re-take the licensing exam.

questions.question_text: What is the fee for the initial Vermont real estate license application?
questions.explanation: The initial application fee for a Vermont real estate license is $100. The exam fee is an additional $110, and the biennial renewal fee is $220.
